description: Affrication and shift in place of articulation from a bilabial nasal [m] to a voiced alveolar affricate [dz]. This is a very rare sound change and represents a significant articulatory shift.

languageExamples.description: This transformation is not a common or regular sound change and would be considered an unusual dialectal or idiolectal feature.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
